title: astragalus bisontum var. minor
intro: Astragalus bisontum var. minor, also known as the "small western milkvetch," is a perennial plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to the western United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to support the immune system and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Astragalus bisontum var. minor include its ability to enhance immune function, reduce stress and fatigue, and improve cardiovascular health, making it a popular herb in traditional Chinese medicine. Astragalus bisontum var. minor can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, capsules, and powders, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote a healthy immune system.
